zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Beaumont於1296年出生在英國英格蘭戴文時，他的父親William de Beaumont I28歲，母親Elizabeth de Lacy25歲。他和Annabelle de Lacy至少有1個兒子。他去世在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Beaumont于1296年出生在英国英格兰戴文时，他的父亲William de Beaumont I28岁，母亲Elizabeth de Lacy25岁。他和Annabelle de Lacy至少有1个儿子。他去世在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Beaumont was born in 1296, in Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, William de Beaumont I, was 28 and his mother, Elizabeth de Lacy, was 25. He had at least 1 son with Annabelle de Lacy. He died in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Beaumont est né en 1296, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Beaumont I, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Lacy, avait 25 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Annabelle de Lacy. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Beaumont 1296 in Devon, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Beaumont I 28 und seine Mutter Elizabeth de Lacy 25. Er hatte mit Annabelle de Lacy mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Beaumont è nato il nel 1296 nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William de Beaumont I, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth de Lacy, aveva 25 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Annabelle de Lacy. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.Devon, England, United Kingdom: イギリスイギリスデボン
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Beaumontが1296年にイギリスイギリスデボンで生まれた時，父William de Beaumont Iは28歳，母Elizabeth de Lacyは25歳であった。彼はAnnabelle de Lacyとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Beaumont(이)가 1296년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Beaumont I(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth de Lacy(은)는 25세였다. 그는 Annabelle de Lacy(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Beaumont nasceu em 1296, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William de Beaumont I, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth de Lacy, tinha 25 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Annabelle de Lacy. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Beaumont родился в 1296 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William de Beaumont I было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Lacy, 25. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Annabelle de Lacy. Он умер в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Beaumont nació en 1296, en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William de Beaumont I, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Elizabeth de Lacy, tenía 25 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Annabelle de Lacy. Murió en su pueblo.
